title: 4-Drawer Simple Dresser, Narrow Depth
summary: A shallow-depth four-drawer dresser built from 1x12 sides instead of plywood — narrow enough for a tight bedroom wall, with an optional jigsawed arch on the base trim.
description:
At roughly 11" deep, this dresser is built for rooms where a standard 16"-20"-deep dresser just won't fit — a narrow hallway, a small bedroom, a spot beside a doorway. The 1x12 sides keep the depth honest instead of forcing plywood into a width it wasn't meant for.

The stile spacing is the one measurement worth double- and triple-checking: the plan calls for exactly 7-1/2" between the two vertical 1x2 stiles so the drawer faces land right, and that number is unforgiving — get it wrong and every one of the four drawer faces above it inherits the mistake. The drawers themselves sit inset, so remember to install the slides 3/4" back from the face of the dresser to leave room for it.

The decorative arch cut into the bottom front trim is optional and purely cosmetic — skip it for a plainer look with zero structural consequence.

tools.note: Only needed for the optional decorative arch cutout.

cutList.note: One with an optional decorative arch cut into it.
Space exactly 7-1/2" apart so drawer faces fit correctly.

steps.title: Trim the sides
steps.body: Attach the 1x3 trim to the bottom and the 1x2 trim to the top of both side panels with 1-1/4" finish nails and glue.

steps.title: Attach the legs
steps.body: Attach the legs to both sides, using either 3" screws countersunk or pocket holes set for 3/4" stock with 1-1/4" pocket hole screws.

steps.title: Attach the rails
steps.body: Attach the front and back 1x3 rails flush to the outsides of the sides. If you're adding the optional decorative arch, cut it into the bottom front rail with a jigsaw before attaching.

steps.title: Attach the face frame stiles
steps.body: Attach the two 1x2 stiles carefully — leave exactly 7-1/2" between them so the drawer faces fit correctly. This measurement matters more than almost any other in the build.

steps.title: Build and attach the top
steps.body: If you have a pocket-hole jig, build the top assembly with pocket holes and attach it to the dresser. Otherwise, attach support cleats first, then attach the top pieces with 1-1/4" finish nails and glue. Overhang the top 1" on the front and sides.

steps.title: Build and install the drawer boxes
steps.body: Build the four drawer boxes and nail the plywood bottoms in place. Install using drawer slides — since the drawer faces sit inset, install the slides 3/4" back from the face of the dresser.

steps.title: Attach the drawer faces and hardware
steps.body: With all drawers installed, lay the dresser on its back. Center each drawer face on its box with an even 1/8" gap on all sides, and attach with a few 1-1/4" finish nails and glue — keep an eye on where your hardware will go so you don't nail through a knob location. Once faces are set, pull the drawers back out and reinforce from the inside with 1-1/4" fasteners, then add the hardware.

images.alt: A narrow-depth four-drawer dresser built from solid 1x12 sides
